mission.title: cri-o: Files included in CentOS RPM interfere with CNI operation
mission.description: Files included in CentOS RPM interfere with CNI operation. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cri-o deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cri-o version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
helm list -n cri-o 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cri-o installation.

steps.title: Review cri-o configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cri-o configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get configmap -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cri-o
```
**Description**

When I deploy Calico CNI to together with crio, the overlay network doesn't work at all.

1. Install crio on nodes
2. Deploy calico

**Describe the results you received:**

crio itself seems to be working.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Files included in CentOS RPM interfere with CNI operation
steps.description:
ah yes this makes sense. I suppose the solution would be to separate the CNI plugin installation from CRI-O, and have the CNI plugin installation optional. We would also need to update documentation to make sure users see how best to install the bridge plugin if they're looking for that.

out of
```yaml
$ rpm -qf  /etc/cni/net.d/100-crio-bridge.conf
cri-o-1.14.9-1.el7.x86_64
$ rpm -qf  /etc/cni/net.d/200-loopback.conf
cri-o-1.14.9-1.el7.x86_64
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get events -n cri-o --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Files included in CentOS RPM interfere with CNI operation" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
ah yes this makes sense. I suppose the solution would be to separate the CNI plugin installation from CRI-O, and have the CNI plugin installation optional. We would also need to update documentation to make sure users see how best to install the bridge plugin if they're looking for that.

out of curiosity, what operating system are you installing on  ?
codeSnippets:
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"

HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"

$ k get pods -A -o wide

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cri-o installed or the issue environment reproducible.
